findings: num_kv_splits changes generated output on this stack: five split counts produced five completion lengths (64/80/54/72/72) at temperature 0, each internally deterministic across five runs. --enable-deterministic-inference therefore guarantees reproducibility only WITHIN a fixed split count.
The variable-length sweep ranked 80 splits best at +7.6%. Under equal work it is the WORST measured arm. The confound did not merely add noise, it inverted the ranking.
Arms 80 and 96 rose monotonically across all five runs (45.1% and 57.4% spread) and never reached steady state; their medians are not admissible and those two split counts remain unresolved.
Among converged arms (48, 64, 128 at 1.2-2.0% spread), 64 is best: 56.334 tok/s versus 52.950 (-6.0%) and 54.869 (-2.6%).
Output identity across arms was NOT established: decode_ab records only a 60-character sample prefix, which is insufficient to prove two 80-token generations match.

64.note: monotonically increasing across all runs; not at steady state, median not admissible

80.note: monotonically increasing across all runs; not at steady state, median not admissible

96.note: monotonically increasing across all runs; not at steady state, median not admissible

method.runs: one cold prime, then five hot runs; median of run-level average TPOT
method.why_ignore_eos: num_kv_splits reorders the flash-decode reduction and moves the EOS point at temperature 0, so arms otherwise decode different lengths

next: Re-measure 80 and 96 with enough warm-up runs to reach steady state before ruling on them.
Have decode_ab record a full-output hash so output preservation is checkable rather than assumed.
question: Did patch 086's num_kv_splits=64 optimum move once native block-FP8 cut dense work?

server.override: SGLANG_KV_SPLITS_OVERRIDE, verified by reading triton_attention_num_kv_splits back from /get_server_info per arm

superseded_variable_length_arms.note: INVALID - each arm decoded a different generation length, so the arms did not do equal work. Retained because the divergence is itself the finding.

verdict: No change. 64 remains the best converged arm; no promotion.
